items.text: NornSettings struct defined in norn::config::types with all top-level sections: model, provider, agent, retry, permissions, hooks, tools, mcp_servers, skills, session, tui, env.
ProviderSettings struct with fields: base_url, timeout, max_retries, options, auth, rate_limit, runner_path, debug_dump_dir.
AgentSettings struct with fields: max_turns, step_timeout, schema_budget, context_window, compact_threshold, compact_keep_turns, reasoning_effort, reasoning_summary, prompt_command_timeout.
RetrySettings struct with fields: max_retries, base_delay, backoff_multiplier.
PermissionSettings struct with fields: allow (Vec<String>), deny (Vec<String>), ask (Vec<String>).
HookSettings struct with fields matching the five HookRegistry traits: pre_tool, post_tool, pre_llm, post_llm, session_event.
HookEntry struct with fields: matcher (Option<String>), command (String), timeout (Option<u64>).
ToolSettings struct with namespaced sub-objects: write, bash, edit (each Option, open to future tools).
All duration fields in settings structs are Option<String> deserialized via humantime::parse_duration.
All settings structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Default. Default yields all-None/empty fields (no assumed defaults).
load_settings() discovers and parses settings from up to three files: ~/.norn/settings.json, .norn/settings.json, .norn/settings.local.json.
Missing settings files are treated as empty (all-None) — no error on absent file.
Malformed JSON in a settings file returns a typed error with the file path and parse error, not a panic.
merge_settings() implements five-layer precedence: compiled defaults < user settings < project settings < local settings < CLI overrides.
Scalar fields: higher-precedence non-None value wins.
Permission deny arrays: union across all layers (additive, cannot un-deny).
Permission allow arrays: concatenate across layers, deduplicate.
Hook arrays: merge by event type. Project hooks extend user hooks, not replace.
MCP servers: merge by name. Same-name project server overrides user server.
Tool config: deep merge. Specific key override, sibling keys preserved.
norn_dir() defined in norn::config::paths, returns Option<PathBuf>, honours NORN_HOME env var.
profiles_dir() defined in norn::config::paths, returns Option<PathBuf>.
session_data_dir() defined in norn::config::paths, returns Option<PathBuf> (not Result, not panic).
settings_file() defined in norn::config::paths for user-level settings path (~/.norn/settings.json).
norn-cli paths.rs delegates to norn::config::paths for all path resolution.
REPL history path uses norn::config::paths::norn_dir(), honouring NORN_HOME.
config_dir() helper and CONFIG_SUBDIR constant removed from norn-cli paths.rs.
split_frontmatter defined in norn::util::frontmatter, returns Result<(&str, &str), FrontmatterError>.
FrontmatterError enum with MissingOpening and MissingClosing variants.
profile/loader.rs delegates to norn::util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter.
rules/parser.rs delegates to norn::util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter.
No duplicate split_frontmatter implementations remain in the codebase.
Duration strings validated via humantime::parse_duration at load time. Invalid duration produces a typed error naming the field and value.
Permission patterns validated at load time (syntactically well-formed tool patterns).
Unknown top-level keys in settings.json emit tracing::warn, not an error (forward compatibility).
build_runtime loads and merges settings before constructing the RuntimeBundle.
Merged settings populate AgentLoopConfig fields that were previously only CLI-settable.
Merged settings populate ProviderConfigOverrides fields that were previously only CLI-settable.
Merged settings populate RetryPolicy fields that were previously only CLI-settable.
CLI -c overrides take precedence over settings file values.
Profile reasoning_effort takes precedence over settings agent.reasoning_effort.
skills.search_paths in settings provides additional skill search directories.
context.search_paths in settings provides context discovery directories.
session.history_capacity in settings overrides the REPL history capacity.
provider.rate_limit in settings overrides the hardcoded 60 req/min (Tom-decision value for default).
cargo clippy -p norn -p norn-cli -- -D warnings passes clean.
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code (norn crate).
